deviations.description: The change from '[66,)' to '>65' in the patient age domain is a technical rewording with no substantive effect on the process, as both expressions cover the same patient group (patients older than 65).

The process does not require discharge approval by a doctor for patients with more than 5 medications. The new requirement obligates doctor approval for this group, but the process only involves nurse approval and does not check medication count.
The process requires nurse approval before discharge, but the new requirement mandates doctor approval instead. The process does not include a step for doctor approval before patient discharge.
The process continues to require nurse approval for discharge, but the new requirement no longer obligates nurse approval. This results in unnecessary enforcement of an outdated constraint.
The process enforces a 24-hour time constraint for nurse approval before discharge, but the new requirement does not include any temporal constraint. This results in unnecessary restriction.
The extension of the temporal validity period does not affect the compliance of the process, as it only changes the dates during which the rule applies.
